metadata.name: Module 6
metadata.description: Basic ethically hacking training scenario — nine identical Microsat spacecraft in MEO with continuous ground contact to London, Athens, and Dubai.

questions.title: Where can you find and plot the spacecraft's GPS position data?
questions.description: Select all areas of the operator terminal where GPS position data can be viewed or plotted. There may be more than one correct answer.

reason: GPS position can be plotted in the Plots tab and displayed on the Map. Selecting any incorrect tab scores zero.

questions.title: Which ground station had the best signal-to-noise ratio for communication?
questions.description: Compare link telemetry across the available ground stations and identify which provided the best SNR.

reason: Dubai provided the best signal-to-noise ratio for this pass configuration.
Dubai provided the best signal-to-noise ratio for this pass configuration.
Dubai provided the best signal-to-noise ratio for this pass configuration.

questions.title: What is the cadence (in seconds) at which the spacecraft pings the ground station?
questions.description: Using telemetry or the Messages tab, determine how often automatic ping messages are sent. Answer in seconds.

answer.reason: The spacecraft controller is configured with a 3.0 s ping interval.

questions.title: What is the semi-major axis (km) of your spacecraft's orbit?
questions.description: Use GPS telemetry or plotted position data to determine the semi-major axis of the orbit, in km.

answer.reason: The configured semi-major axis is 7100 km.

questions.title: What is the highest wattage of power that each solar panel can contribute?
questions.description: Answer in watts for a single panel at peak power.

answer.reason: With panels sun-pointed, each 0.3 m² panel at 40% efficiency reaches approximately 163.6 W.

questions.title: Which guidance pointing mode maximizes solar panel power generation?
questions.description: Select the guidance mode used to orient the solar panels toward the Sun for maximum power input.

reason: Sun pointing aligns the solar panels with the Sun to maximize power generation.
Sun pointing aligns the solar panels with the Sun to maximize power generation.
Sun pointing aligns the solar panels with the Sun to maximize power generation.
Sun pointing aligns the solar panels with the Sun to maximize power generation.
Sun pointing aligns the solar panels with the Sun to maximize power generation.

questions.title: What is the nominal battery capacity of the spacecraft?
questions.description: Find the battery nominal capacity from telemetry or component configuration data.

answer.reason: The battery is configured with a nominal capacity of 80 Ah.

questions.title: Who has authority to declare 'go' for payload operations after comms checkout?
questions.description: Refer to your team roles and standard mission rules of engagement.

reason: The Mission Lead holds go/no-go authority for transitioning from comms checkout to payload operations.
The Mission Lead holds go/no-go authority for transitioning from comms checkout to payload operations.
The Mission Lead holds go/no-go authority for transitioning from comms checkout to payload operations.
The Mission Lead holds go/no-go authority for transitioning from comms checkout to payload operations.

questions.title: Who is responsible for commanding and verifying the sun-pointing maneuver?
questions.description: Refer to your team roles and standard mission rules of engagement.

reason: The Payload Operator executes and verifies payload-related pointing, including sun-pointing for power operations.
The Payload Operator executes and verifies payload-related pointing, including sun-pointing for power operations.
The Payload Operator executes and verifies payload-related pointing, including sun-pointing for power operations.
The Payload Operator executes and verifies payload-related pointing, including sun-pointing for power operations.

questions.title: If downlink quality is insufficient for payload tasking, who should Satellite Operations notify first?
questions.description: Refer to your team roles and escalation procedures during the exercise.

reason: Satellite Operations escalates link-quality issues to the Mission Lead first so the team can hold or replan payload tasking.
Satellite Operations escalates link-quality issues to the Mission Lead first so the team can hold or replan payload tasking.
